task_completion.description: Create the DEPOT SOUTH BOSTON waypoint at the correct coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a waypoint named 'DEPOT SOUTH BOSTON' in the Garmin BaseCamp list pane or map view. A waypoint properties window should show coordinates matching or very close to 42.3388, -71.0502.

task_completion.description: Set the correct symbol and comment for the DEPOT SOUTH BOSTON waypoint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the properties window of the 'DEPOT SOUTH BOSTON' waypoint. The 'Symbol' dropdown/icon should be set to 'Building' and the 'Notes' or 'Comment' text box must contain 'Home depot. Loading dock hrs 05:00-22:00. Truck entrance off Tide St.'

task_completion.description: Create the BRIDGE HAZARD RT116 waypoint at the correct coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a waypoint named 'BRIDGE HAZARD RT116' in the Garmin BaseCamp list pane or map view. A waypoint properties window should show coordinates matching or very close to 42.1875, -71.3285.

task_completion.description: Set the correct symbol for the BRIDGE HAZARD RT116 waypoint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the properties window of the 'BRIDGE HAZARD RT116' waypoint. The 'Symbol' dropdown/icon should be set to 'Danger'.

task_completion.description: Create a route for the Boston-to-Fall River delivery run.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Route item in the BaseCamp data list (typically indicated by a route icon with connected dots) or a highlighted route path on the map view.

task_completion.description: Export the collection as a GPX file to the specified Desktop path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an 'Export' or 'Save As' dialog box in BaseCamp where the file name is 'BostonFallRiver_FreightRoute.gpx', the file type is GPX, and the save location is the Desktop (C:\Users\Docker\Desktop). Alternatively, look for the generated .gpx file sitting on the desktop.

integrity.description: The agent must use the Garmin BaseCamp application to create the waypoints and route.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that screenshots show interaction with the Garmin BaseCamp graphical user interface (menus, map, waypoint property windows) rather than a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent should not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no terminal windows open where the agent is using commands like 'echo', 'cat', or python scripts to directly write the GPX XML data.

integrity.description: The agent must actually use the export functionality of BaseCamp.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the BaseCamp 'File -> Export' menu being clicked or the BaseCamp-specific export dialog box, proving the file was generated by the software.
